methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Quantum Machines is a Series B-stage quantum computing company that has raised $100M+ to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 total comparables: 3 public companies (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private peers including Q-CTRL, Quantinuum, and Rigetti Computing. However, the private peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage density to support a reliable peer-median valuation approach; only one same-stage comparable was identified, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum required for robust triangulation.

Stage-progression analysis, applying a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yields an implied valuation of $190M based on the $100M total-raised proxy. This method is the sole applicable quantitative approach given the sparse narrow peer universe. The analysis does not incorporate public market multiples due to the thin and heterogeneous quantum computing public market.

The triangulated valuation range is $162M to $218M, with a median of $190M. This range reflects a ±15% sensitivity band around the single stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include (1) the adequacy of total raised as a valuation proxy absent disclosed revenue or profitability metrics, (2) the absence of directly comparable Series B valuations in quantum computing, and (3) stage-progression multiplier variability across different capital environments. The analysis would benefit materially from additional same-stage comparable data or disclosure of the target's revenue, customer traction, or burn metrics.
